Discussed, not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SANTOS & URRUTIA ASSOCIATES, INC.'S Motion For Order Determining Good Faith Settlement.

Defendants Santos & Urrutia Associates, Inc. and Michael Chan's motion for order determining good faith settlement is denied without prejudice to the settling parties renewing the motion upon the completion of reasonable discovery by non-settling parties directed to the Tech-Bilt factors. (Singer Co. v. Superior Court (1986) 179 Cal.App.3d 875, 897 [trial court's refusal to postpone hearing hearing on good faith of proposed settlement with plaintiffs until such time as objecting non-settling party could complete pertinent discovery was abuse of discretion]; see also City of Grand Terrace v. Superior Court (1987) 192 Cal.App.3d 1251, 1265; Rankin v. Curtis (1986) 183 Cal.App.3d 939, 948.)
